design_philosophy.core_loop: Call play, execute offensive/defensive strategy, manage field position across four quarters of football simulation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing strategic play-calling with real-time execution; adapting to opponent's defense/offense
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Coach and execute as a real NFL team with authentic rosters, logos, and uniforms; master authentic football strategy
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First Madden game with official NFL team license, establishing authenticity as core design pillar. Prioritizes realistic football simulation over arcade accessibility. Introduces 'Play-call mode' and 'Bluff play' mechanics to reward tactical decision-making. Converts previous generic players/teams to licensed NFL rosters, fundamentally shifting franchise toward simulation fidelity.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects difficulty through play-calling strategy and opponent selection; game supports single-player and multiplayer modes allowing variable challenge levels based on opponent skill

progression.unlock_mechanism: Super Bowl teams available for play without special codes (first in series)
replayability_hooks: Multiple NFL teams with different rosters and play styles
Instant replay with rotatable camera angles for analysis
Season play structure across full NFL calendar

world_building_techniques: Authentic NFL team branding and uniforms from 1993-1994 season
Licensed team logos creating real-world sports context

faction_indicators: 32 distinct NFL teams representing regional identity and allegiance
Official team logos and uniforms signaling franchise loyalty
environmental_storytelling: Authentic stadiums and field layouts reflecting real NFL venues
Season progression tracking current NFL calendar (1993-1994)

sources_used: wikipedia - Madden NFL '94 article (gameplay mechanics, development, reception)
